questionText: A particle moves along the path $x = 2e^{t}$ and $y = e^{2t}$, where distances are in metres and $t \ge 0$. Find the coordinates of the point on the path where the tangent is parallel to the line $y = 4x - 1$.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: The exponential $e^{t}$ differentiates to itself, so the horizontal velocity keeps the same form.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Using the chain rule on $e^{2t}$ brings down the factor of $2$ from the exponent.

steps.description: Form the gradient of the path
steps.explanation: Dividing the two rates and using $e^{2t}/e^{t} = e^{t}$ gives a neat expression for the slope of the path at any time.
For the tangent to be parallel to the given line, its gradient must equal $4$.
This equation fixes the value of the parameter at which the path points in the required direction.
Taking natural logs undoes the exponential, giving the exact time. Since $\ln 4 > 0$, it lies in the allowed range $t \ge 0$.
Because $e^{\ln 4} = 4$, the exponential and logarithm cancel to give a clean value.
Using $2\ln 4 = \ln 4^{2} = \ln 16$, the exponential cancels the logarithm to leave $16$.
This is the point on the path where the tangent runs parallel to the line $y = 4x - 1$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The tangent is parallel to $y = 4x - 1$ at the point $(8,\, 16)$, which occurs at $t = \ln 4$.
